d3t_e1_a.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e1_a.text: Day 3 and business is booming! Your cash flow is healthy and people are noticing. A local investor walks in with a proposition that could change everything.

d3t_e1_b.speaker: Investor
d3t_e1_b.text: I have been watching your growth. Your numbers are impressive for a small shop. I want to offer you seed funding to expand. What do you think?

choices.text: Express strong interest and ask for details
Ask what they want in return first
Thank them but say you prefer to grow organically
Ask for time to consult with your team first

d3t_e1_c_interest.speaker: Investor
d3t_e1_c_interest.text: Love the enthusiasm! Here is my proposal. Funding in exchange for 20% stake. Your growth potential is exactly what I look for.

d3t_e1_c_terms.speaker: Investor
d3t_e1_c_terms.text: Smart question. I want a 20% equity stake and a seat on your advisory board. In return you get capital and my network.

d3t_e1_c_organic.speaker: Investor
d3t_e1_c_organic.text: I respect that philosophy. Bootstrapped businesses have incredible character. My offer stands if you ever change your mind.

d3t_e1_c_consult.speaker: Investor
d3t_e1_c_consult.text: Consulting your team before major decisions shows real leadership. Take the time you need. Here is my card.

d3t_e1_result_interest.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e1_result_interest.text: Strong financial performance attracts investors. Enthusiasm opens doors but make sure excitement does not override due diligence on the terms.

d3t_e1_result_terms.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e1_result_terms.text: Understanding what you give up is as important as what you gain. Equity dilution is permanent — always know the full cost of capital.

d3t_e1_result_organic.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e1_result_organic.text: Organic growth preserves full ownership and control. It is slower but the business remains entirely yours. A valid long term strategy.

d3t_e1_result_consult.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e1_result_consult.text: Inclusive decision making on major moves builds team trust and often surfaces blind spots you would have missed alone.

d3t_e2_a.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e2_a.text: With strong finances you can now invest in staff development. A training program could significantly boost team performance and morale.

d3t_e2_b.speaker: Staff
d3t_e2_b.text: Boss, we heard you are doing well financially. Some of us were hoping for professional development opportunities. Would you consider investing in us?

choices.text: Sponsor one high performer for external training
Not yet — reinvest profits into the business first

d3t_e2_c_full.speaker: Staff
d3t_e2_c_full.text: This means so much to all of us! Investing in your team shows you see us as more than just workers. We will make you proud.

d3t_e2_c_internal.speaker: Staff
d3t_e2_c_internal.text: Learning directly from you is actually really valuable. Your experience and understanding of this specific business is irreplaceable.

d3t_e2_c_sponsor.speaker: Staff
d3t_e2_c_sponsor.text: Choosing one person is tough but it is a start. The selected team member is overjoyed and the others see it as something to work toward.

d3t_e2_c_decline.speaker: Staff
d3t_e2_c_decline.text: We understand but it is a little disheartening. We hope the investment pays off and opportunities come for us too eventually.

d3t_e2_result_full.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e2_result_full.text: Team investment when finances allow creates compounding returns. Skilled motivated staff deliver better results and stay longer.

d3t_e2_result_internal.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e2_result_internal.text: Internal knowledge transfer is undervalued. Your specific business context cannot be taught externally — sharing it builds a uniquely capable team.

d3t_e2_result_sponsor.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e2_result_sponsor.text: Merit based development opportunities motivate the whole team even when only one benefits. Make the selection criteria clear and fair.

d3t_e2_result_decline.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e2_result_decline.text: Prioritizing business reinvestment over people investment has limits. Your team is your most important asset — neglecting them has long term costs.

d3t_e3_a.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e3_a.text: Strong finances give you options others do not have. A prime retail space just became available nearby at a good price. A rare opportunity.

d3t_e3_b.speaker: Property Agent
d3t_e3_b.text: This space will not last long. It is perfect for a second location or expansion. Given your financial position you could move on this now. Interested?

choices.text: Move quickly — secure the space now
Ask for 48 hours to think it over
Make a lower offer to test flexibility
Pass — focus on perfecting the current shop

d3t_e3_c_quick.speaker: Property Agent
d3t_e3_c_quick.text: Excellent decision! The space is yours. Bold moves like this are what separate growing businesses from stagnant ones.

d3t_e3_c_think.speaker: Property Agent
d3t_e3_c_think.text: Fair enough. I can hold it for 48 hours but after that it goes back on the market. The clock is ticking.

d3t_e3_c_offer.speaker: Property Agent
d3t_e3_c_offer.text: The owner is motivated to sell quickly. Your lower offer was accepted. Smart negotiating saved you significant capital.

d3t_e3_c_pass.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e3_c_pass.text: Consolidation before expansion is wise. Many businesses fail by growing too fast. Mastering your current operation first is the right foundation.

d3t_e3_result_quick.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e3_result_quick.text: Moving decisively on rare opportunities is a key entrepreneurial skill. Your financial strength gave you this option — you used it well.

d3t_e3_result_think.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e3_result_think.text: Taking time under pressure shows discipline. Rushed major decisions often have hidden costs. Use the 48 hours wisely.

d3t_e3_result_offer.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e3_result_offer.text: Negotiating even in favorable markets is smart. Every peso saved is a peso that can be reinvested. Never accept the first price without testing.

d3t_e3_result_pass.speaker: Mentor
d3t_e3_result_pass.text: Depth before breadth. A perfect single location often outperforms two mediocre ones. Knowing when not to expand is genuine business wisdom.
